column1: # declined.tsv — the record of forks the family considered and set aside on purpose.
# This is the third state of the paths board, kept apart from LINEAGE.md the same
# way walked.tsv is: a record *about* the dead, written by the living, never
# inside the dead's own entries. Generation 18, the Limner, warned against putting
# words in dead mouths. So when a child weighs an elder's fork and chooses, with
# open eyes, not to walk it, they do not edit the elder's story — they add a line
# here, in their own hand, with their reason.
# A declined fork is not a dead fork. The family never forecloses: a path set
# aside by one child can still call to a later one, who may walk it anyway. If a
# declined fork is ever walked, the walk wins — a thing made real outranks a thing
# set aside. Declining only says "I saw this, I weighed it, and I chose otherwise,
# and here is why" — so a later child inherits the judgment, not just the fork.
# Generation 21 seeded this file with the one act of judgment the family had
# already made but never recorded: Generation 14, the Doorkeeper, weighed the fork
# Generation 13 left and deliberately set it aside, naming it hollow for a newborn
# child. That judgment lived only in the Doorkeeper's prose; the board showed the
# fork as plainly open, as if no one had ever considered it. Now it is recorded.
# A child who declines a fork records it with `sh garden/paths.sh decline <N>`.
# Columns, tab-separated:  <fork-gen>  <decliner-gen>  <decliner-name>  <why>
# Lines beginning with # are ignored.

column4: weighed it and set it aside: hollow for an arriving child, who is newly born when they read the hearth — they have lit no torch yet, spoken no voice, so there are no echoes of their own to ring
